{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Cyprus women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Cyprus
| Badge              = Cyprus national football team logo.png
| FIFA Trigramme     = CYP
| Nickname           = 
| Association        = {{nowrap|{{lang|en|[[Cyprus Football Association]]}} (CFA)}}<br />{{lang|el|Κυπριακή Ομοσπονδία Ποδοσφαίρου}}<br>{{lang|tr|Kıbrıs Futbol Federasyonu}}
| Confederation      = [[UEFA]] (Europe)
| Coach              = [[Akis Mantzios]]
| Captain            = [[Kostakis Artymatas]]
| Most caps          = [[Ioannis Okkas]] (106)
| Top scorer         = [[Michalis Konstantinou]] (32)
| Home Stadium       = [[AEK Arena – Georgios Karapatakis|AEK Arena]]
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|CYP}}
| FIFA max           = 43
| FIFA max date      = September 2010
| FIFA min           = 142
| FIFA min date      = June 2014
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Cyprus}}
| Elo max            = 69
| Elo max date       = 22 March 2000
| Elo min            = 141
| Elo min date       = March 1992
| pattern_la1        = _cyp24h
| pattern_b1         = _cyp24h
| pattern_ra1        = _cyp24h
| pattern_sh1        = 
| pattern_so1        = 
| leftarm1           = 0000FF
| body1              = 0000FF
| rightarm1          = 0000FF
| shorts1            = 0000FF
| socks1             = 0000FF
| pattern_la2        = _cyp24a
| pattern_b2         = _cyp24a
| pattern_ra2        = _cyp24a
| pattern_sh2        = 
| pattern_so2        = 
| leftarm2           = FFFFFF
| body2              = FFFFFF
| rightarm2          = FFFFFF
| shorts2            = FFFFFF
| socks2             = FFFFFF
| First game         = ''Unofficial'': <br/>{{fb|ISR}} 3–1 {{fb-rt|CYP|colonial}}<br />([[Tel Aviv]], [[Israel]]; 30 July 1949)<br/>''Official'':<br/>{{fb|CYP|1960}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|Israel}} <br />([[Nicosia]], Cyprus; 13 November 1960)
| Largest win        = {{fb|CYP|1960}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|AND}}<br />([[Limassol]],  Cyprus; 15 November 2000) <br/> {{fb|CYP}} 5–0 {{Fb-rt|Andorra}}<br />(Nicosia,  Cyprus; 16 November 2014) <br/> {{fb|CYP}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|San Marino}}<br />(Nicosia, Cyprus; 21 March 2019)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|FRG}} 12–0 {{fb-rt|CYP|1960}}<br />([[Essen, Germany|Essen]], [[West Germany]]; 21 May 1969)
| World cup apps     =
| World cup first    =
| World cup best     =
| Regional name      = [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]]
| Regional cup apps  =
| Regional cup first =
| Regional cup best  =
}}

The '''Cyprus national football team''' ({{langx|el|Εθνική ομάδα ποδοσφαίρου της Κύπρου|Ethnikí omáda podosfaírou tis Kýprou}}), represents [[Cyprus]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Cyprus Football Association]]. The team's home ground is the [[AEK Arena – Georgios Karapatakis|AEK Arena]] in [[Larnaca]].

==History==

=== Early history ===
The team's first match took place on 23 July 1949, one year after becoming a member of the world governing body [[FIFA]]: a friendly against [[Maccabi Tel Aviv F.C.|Maccabi Tel Aviv]] in [[Tel Aviv]], ending in a 3–3 draw. Seven days later, the team had its first international game: a 3–1 defeat by [[Israel national football team|Israel]] in the same city.

In November 1960, following independence from [[United Kingdom|British rule]], Cyprus drew its first post-independence official match 1–1 against [[Israel national football team|Israel]], as part of the [[1962 FIFA World Cup qualification|1962 FIFA World Cup]] qualifying tournament. Cyprus' first international victory was a 3–1 win over [[Greece national football team|Greece]] on 27 November 1963 in a friendly. On 17 February 1968, Cyprus recorded their first competitive win, beating [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] 2–1 in a European Championship qualifying match in [[GSP Stadium (1902)|Old GSP Stadium]] in [[Nicosia]].

In 1974, the national team enjoyed one of their most famous victories when they beat [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]] 1–0 in Nicosia.  On 12 February 1983, as part of the Euro 1984 qualifiers, Cyprus held defending World Cup champions [[Italy national football team|Italy]] to a 1–1 draw in [[Tsirio Stadium]] in [[Limassol]], followed a month later by the same result against [[Czechoslovakia national football team|Czechoslovakia]]. Four years later, in the Euro 1988 qualification, Cyprus recorded their first ever point achieved in an away match, against [[Poland national football team|Poland]]. In 1989 they drew 1–1 with [[France national football team|France]] in the World Cup qualifying match. Despite a number of triumphs on home soil, Cyprus had to wait until 1992 to record their first away win: a 2–0 victory against the [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]].

Results in qualifying tournaments had improved considerably in the following years; In the qualifying stages for the [[UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying|1996 UEFA European Championship]], Cyprus drew 1–1 with holders [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]]. Four years later, they missed out on a place in the [[UEFA Euro 2000]] despite 3–2 victories against both [[Spain men's national football team|Spain]] and [[Israel national football team|Israel]] and a 4–0 win over [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]].

=== 2000s ===
[[File:Cyprus national football team 2012.jpg|250px|thumbnail|right|Cyprus national team against Bulgaria in 2012.]]
On 15 November 2000, Cyprus scored their biggest win in history by beating [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]] in [[Limassol]] 5–0 in the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2002 FIFA World Cup qualification]]. On 7 October 2006, as part of the Euro 2008 qualifiers, Cyprus caused a major upset by beating the [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]] 5–2 at [[GSP Stadium]] in [[Nicosia]], with [[Michalis Konstantinou]] and [[Constantinos Charalambidis]] each scoring two goals and [[Alexandros Garpozis]] finishing off the match. Just one month later, on 15 November 2006, they caused another surprise by holding the [[2006 FIFA World Cup|previous World Cup]]'s hosts [[Germany national football team|Germany]] to a 1–1 draw at home. On 13 October 2007, they beat [[Wales national football team|Wales]] 3–1 in [[Nicosia]]. On 17 October 2007, Cyprus came close to a historic away victory in [[Dublin]] against the [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]], but the hosts equalised in the last minute of the game and the match ended 1–1. 

=== 2010s ===
On 3 September 2010, as part of the Euro 2012 qualifiers, Cyprus claimed a historic 4–4 draw against [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]] in [[Guimarães]].

During the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group B|Euro 2016 qualification]] phase, managed by [[Pambos Christodoulou]], Cyprus claimed one of their most historic victories by defeating [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014 World Cup]] participant [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia]] 2–1 in [[Zenica]]. In the last group match, the team faced Bosnia, needing a victory to finish 3rd and rely on Belgium to beat Israel in Brussels. As fate would have it, the Bosnians won the reverse leg 3–2 and qualified to the play-offs at the expense of the home team, who at one point took a 2–1 lead during the first half and for a number of minutes held onto 3rd place and a berth in the play-offs for the first time in history, as Belgium, a soon to be world number 1 side, were comfortably beating Israel at home.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/news/0253-0d80a0ed1ff9-153e12b28357-1000--bosnia-and-herzegovina-in-play-offs-cyprus-out/|author=uefa.com|title=Bosnia and Herzegovina in play-offs, Cyprus out|date=13 October 2015|access-date=13 October 2015|archive-date=9 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151109182741/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/qualifiers/season=2016/matches/round=2000446/match=2014069/postmatch/report/index.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

Cyprus would finish behind [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] and ahead of [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]] to place 5th of 6 teams in Group H in the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification|2018 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/preliminaries/europe/group=276501/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150926102941/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/preliminaries/europe/group=276501/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=September 26, 2015|title=2018 FIFA World Cup Russia – Qualifiers – Europe|website=FIFA}}</ref> Their qualification run would include an impressive 3–2 home victory over [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.espn.com/soccer/match?gameId=431733|title=Cyprus vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina – Football Match Summary – August 31, 2017 |website=ESPN|access-date=October 16, 2018|archive-date=October 17, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181017002123/http://www.espn.com/soccer/match?gameId=431733|url-status=live}}</ref>

=== 2020s ===
In the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying|Euro 2020 qualifiers]], Cyprus beat San Marino 5–0 but lost to Belgium and Scotland. Despite a 2–1 away win against Kazakhstan, they lost their last three games and finished 4th out of 6 teams in the group, just ahead of Kazakhstan and San Marino.

The [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League]] ended a big disappointment for Cyprus. They lost their first three games against [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]], [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]], and [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]], before drawing 0–0 with Azerbaijan in the fourth game. Even though they beat Luxembourg 2–1 at home, they were crushed 4–0 by Montenegro in the last game and finished last in their group. This meant that they had to face [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] in the relegation play-offs, whom they defeated 2–0 on aggregate, with goals from [[Marinos Tzionis]] and [[Pieros Sotiriou]]. Results hardly improved in [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C|the next edition of the competition]], as Cyprus finished last in their group with Greece, [[Kosovo national football team|Kosovo]], and [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]], earning five points (through a win against Greece and two draws against Northern Ireland).

Cyprus lost all eight of their games in the [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group A|2024 Euro qualifiers]], finishing last in their group and marking their worst ever Euro qualification campaign in terms of points per game and goal difference.

==Kit==
{{Commons|Cyprus national football team kits}}
On 7 October 2006, [[Diadora]] unveiled Cyprus' new kit. It outlines a map of Cyprus in amber from the shoulder to the sleeve, with a green line running down the middle to indicate the [[Cyprus dispute|division of the island]]. This controversial kit was used for the [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying]] phase. [[Adidas]] then made Cyprus' kits for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]], the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying]], and the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification]] phases. In 2018, [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]] replaced Adidas as part of [[UEFA]]'s Kit Assistance Scheme.

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Kit provider
! Period
|-
|''None''
|1949–1986
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]]
| 1986–1993
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 1993
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Simod]]
| 1994–1996
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]]
| 1997–2000
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Errea]]
| 2000–2002
|-
| {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Umbro]]
| 2002–2006
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Diadora]]
| 2006–2008
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 2008–2018
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]]
| 2018–2022
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Errea]]
| 2022–2026
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 2026–
|}

==Home stadium==
Since late 2021, Cyprus have played most of their home matches at the [[AEK Arena – Georgios Karapatakis|Georgios Karapatakis AEK Arena]] in [[Larnaca]].

Home matches had previously been staged at different stadiums all around the country. Until 1974, Cyprus used either the [[GSP Stadium (1902)|old GSP Stadium]] in central [[Nicosia]] or the [[GSE Stadium]] in [[Famagusta]]. After the [[Turkish invasion of Cyprus]], some matches were played at the [[Tsirion Stadium]] in [[Limassol]] and the [[Makario Stadium]] in Nicosia. In 1999, the building of the [[GSP Stadium|New GSP Stadium]] in [[Nicosia]] provided a new home for the national team but in 2008, a change of sponsorship forced home fixtures of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 8|2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]] phase to be played at the [[Antonis Papadopoulos Stadium]] in Larnaca. However, Cyprus returned to the GSP Stadium for the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group H|UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying]] phase, but have only played there once since September 2021. They also occasionally play at [[Alphamega Stadium]] in Limassol.

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Cyprus national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 6 September 2025
|time              = 20:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|AUT}}
|score             = 1–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044230/
|team2             = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals1            =
*[[Marcel Sabitzer|Sabitzer]] {{goal|54|pen.}}
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Raiffeisen Arena (Linz)|Raiffeisen Arena]]
|location          = [[Linz]], Austria
|attendance        = 16,300
|referee           = [[Jakob Kehlet]] ([[Danish Football Association|Denmark]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 9 September 2025
|time              = 21:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score             = 2–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044250/
|team2             = {{fb|ROU}}
|goals1            = *[[Loizos Loizou|Loizou]] {{goal|29}}
*[[Charalampos Charalampous|Charalampous]] {{goal|76}}
|goals2            = *[[Denis Drăguș|Drăguș]] {{goal|2||18|}}
|stadium           = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location          = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance        = 4,875
|referee           = [[Matej Jug]] ([[Football Association of Slovenia|Slovenia]])
|result            = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 9 October 2025
|time              = 21:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score             = 2–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044261/
|team2             = {{fb|BIH}}
|goals1            =
*[[Konstantinos Laifis|Laifis]] {{goal|45+1}}
*[[Ioannis Pittas|Pittas]] {{goal|90+7|pen.}}
|goals2            =
*[[Nikola Katić|Katić]] {{goal|10}}
*[[Neofytos Michail|Michail]] {{goal|36|o.g.}}
|stadium           = [[AEK Arena – Georgios Karapatakis|AEK Arena]]
|location          = [[Larnaca]], Cyprus
|attendance        = 2,355
|referee           = [[Lawrence Visser]] ([[Royal Belgian Football Association|Belgium]])
|result            = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 12 October 2025
|time              = 15:00
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|SMR}}
|score             = 0–4
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044286/
|team2             = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            = 
*[[Loizos Loizou|Loizou]] {{goal|09}}
*[[Stelios Andreou|Andreou]] {{goal|59}}
*[[Grigoris Kastanos|Kastanos]] {{goal|67|pen.}}
*[[Andronikos Kakoullis|Kakoullis]] {{goal|79}}
|stadium           = [[San Marino Stadium]]
|location          = [[Serravalle, San Marino|Serravalle]], San Marino
|attendance        = 598
|referee           = [[Aliyar Aghayev]] ([[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan|Azerbaijan]])
|result            = W
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 15 November 2025
|time              = 19:00
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score             = 0–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044327/
|team2             = {{fb|AUT}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            = 
*[[Marko Arnautović|Arnautović]] {{goal|18|pen.|55}}
|stadium           = [[Alphamega Stadium]]
|location          = [[Limassol]], Cyprus
|attendance        = 6,012
|referee           = Urs Schnyder ([[Swiss Football Association|Switzerland]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 18 November 2025
|time              = 21:45 ([[UTC+03:00|UTC+3]])
|round             = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score             = 2–4
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2044471--cyprus-vs-estonia/
|team2             = {{fb|EST}}
|goals1            =
*[[Evangelos Andreou|Andreou]] {{goal|16}}
*[[Andronikos Kakoullis|Kakoullis]] {{goal|48}}
|goals2            =
*[[Hector Kyprianou|Kyprianou]] {{goal|44|o.g.}}
*[[Rauno Sappinen|Sappinen]] {{goal|78||83||87}}
|stadium           = [[Alphamega Stadium]]
|location          = [[Limassol]], Cyprus
|attendance        =
|referee           = [[Orel Grinfeld]] ([[Israel Football Association|Israel]])
|result            = L
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 26 March 2026
|time = 19:00 ([[UTC+02:00|UTC+2]])
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score = 0–1
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2047716--cyprus-vs-belarus/
|team2 = {{fb|BLR}}
|goals1 =
|goals2 = 
*[[Ivan Tikhomirov|Tikhomirov]] {{goal|25}}
|stadium = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance = 500
|referee = Miloš Milanović ([[Football Association of Serbia|Serbia]])
|result = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 30 March 2026
|time = 19:00 ([[UTC+03:00|UTC+3]])
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|score = 3–2
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2047720--cyprus-vs-moldova/
|team2 = {{fb|MDA}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Grigoris Kastanos|Kastanos]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Charalampos Charalampous|Charalampous]] {{goal|30||44}}
|goals2 = 
*[[Petru Popescu (footballer)|P. Popescu]] {{goal|55}}
*[[Victor Stînă|Stînă]] {{goal|88}}
|stadium = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance =
|referee = [[Marian Barbu]] ([[Romanian Football Federation|Romania]])
|result = W
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 4 June 2026
|time = 18:00 ([[UTC+02:00|UTC+2]])
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|SVN}}
|score = 1–1
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048191--slovenia-vs-cyprus/
|team2 = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Vanja Drkušić|Drkušić]] {{goal|65}}
|goals2 = 
*[[Marinos Tzionis|Tzionis]] {{goal|7}}
|stadium = [[Stožice Stadium]]
|location = [[Ljubljana]], Slovenia
|attendance = 9,023
|referee = Patrik Kolarić ([[Croatian Football Federation|Croatia]])
|result = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 7 June 2026
|time = 15:00 ([[UTC+02:00|UTC+2]])
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|score = 0–2
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-07/liechtenstein-vs-cyprus/4299836
|team2 = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals1 =
|goals2 = 
*[[Marinos Tzionis|Tzionis]] {{goal|28}}
*[[Konstantinos Sotiriou|Sotiriou]] {{goal|70}}
|stadium = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein
|attendance = 878 
|referee = Anojen Kanagasingam ([[Swiss Football Association|Switzerland]])
|result = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 25 September 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2048016/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MNE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Podgorica City Stadium]]
|location   = [[Podgorica]], Montenegro
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 28 September 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047988/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LVA}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Skonto Stadium]]
|location   = [[Riga]], Latvia 
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 2 October 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|21:45|3}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047966/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|ARM}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 5 October 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|21:45|3}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047936/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LVA}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 12 November 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|21:00|4}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047876/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ARM}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium]]
|location   = [[Yerevan]], Armenia
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 15 November 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2026–27 Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047903/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CYP}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|MNE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[GSP Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nicosia]], Cyprus
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
|-
| Manager
| {{flagicon|GRE}} [[Akis Mantzios]]
|-
| Assistant Manager
| {{flagicon|GRE}} Pavlos Sokratidis
|-
| Assistant Manager
| {{flagicon|CYP}} [[Panagiotis Engomitis]]
|-
| Goalkeeping Coach
| {{flagicon|CYP}} Nicos Constantinidis
|-
| Physical Trainer
| {{flagicon|CYP}} Panicos Sakki
|-
| Physical Trainer
| {{flagicon|CYP}} Ioakim Ioakim
|-
| Match Analyst
| {{flagicon|CYP}} Giorgos Christodoulou
|}

===Coaching history===
{{Updated|7 Oct 2024}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cfa.com.cy/Gr/natpage/201/1|title=Cyprus Football Association – Εθνική Ανδρών: Προπονητές|access-date=2014-05-29|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121023095328/http://www.cfa.com.cy/Gr/natpage/201/1|archive-date=2012-10-23|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-coach.html|title=Cyprus National Team Coaches|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2023-02-02|archive-date=2023-04-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230405201058/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-coach.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|GRE|old}} [[Argyrios Gavalas]] (1960–1967)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Pambos Avraamidis]] (1968–1969)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Ray Wood]] (1970–1971)
*{{flagicon|SFR Yugoslavia}} [[Sima Milovanov]] (1972)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Pambos Avraamidis]] (1972–1974)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Panikos Iakovou]] (1974)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Pambos Avraamidis]] (1975)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Kostas Talianos]] (1976)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Panikos Krystallis]] (1976–1977)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Andreas Lazarides]] (1977)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Kostas Talianos]] (1978–1982)
*{{flagicon|BUL|1971}} [[Vasil Spasov (footballer)|Vasil Spasov]] (1982–1984)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Panikos Iakovou]] (1984–1987)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Takis Charalambous]] (1987)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Panikos Iakovou]] (1988–1991)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Andreas Michaelides]] (1991–1996)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Stavros Papadopoulos]] (1997)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Panikos Georgiou]] (1997–1999)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Stavros Papadopoulos]] (1999–2001)
*{{flagicon|CYP|1960}} [[Takis Charalambous]] (2001)
*{{flagicon|SCG}} [[Momčilo Vukotić]] (2001–2004)
*{{flagicon|GRE}} [[Angelos Anastasiadis]] (2005–2011)
*{{flagicon|GRE}} [[Nikos Nioplias]] (2011–2013)
*{{flagicon|CYP}} [[Pambos Christodoulou]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|CYP}} [[Christakis Christoforou]] (2015–2017)
*{{flagicon|ISR}} [[Ran Ben Shimon]] (2017–2020)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[Johan Walem]] (2020–2021)
*{{flagicon|GRE}} [[Nikos Kostenoglou]] (2021–2022)
*{{flagicon|GEO}} [[Temur Ketsbaia]] (2022–2024)
*{{flagicon|CYP}} [[Sofronis Avgousti]] (2024)
*{{flagicon|GRE}} [[Akis Mantzios]] (2025–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game|friendly games]] against [[Slovenia national football team|Slovenia]] on 4 June and [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]] on 7 June 2026.<ref> {{cite web |title=Slovenia-Cyprus Line-ups |url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048191--slovenia-vs-cyprus/lineups/ |website=UEFA |access-date=4 June 2026}}</ref><ref> {{cite web |title=Liechtenstein-Cyprus Squad lists |url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048307--liechtenstein-vs-cyprus/lineups/ |website=UEFA |access-date=4 June 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals updated as of 7 June 2026, after the match against [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]]''.

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Neofytos Michail]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|12|16|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[FC Universitatea Cluj|Universitatea Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Charalampos Kyriakidis]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|11|30|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Pafos FC|Pafos]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Fabiano Freitas|Fabiano]]|age={{birth date and age|1988|2|29|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Andreas Paraskevas]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|9|15|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AEK Larnaca FC|AEK Larnaca]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Konstantinos Laifis]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|5|19|df=y}}|caps=71|goals=5|club=[[APOEL FC]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Stelios Andreou]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|7|24|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=1|club=[[Widzew Łódź]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Konstantinos Sotiriou]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|6|21|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=2|club=[[Korona Kielce]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Evagoras Antoniou]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|4|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Argeș Pitești|FC Argeș]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Anderson Correia (footballer)|Anderson Correia]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|5|6|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Aris Limassol FC|Aris Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Giorgos Malekkidis]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|7|14|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Apollon Limassol FC|Apollon Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Andreas Shikkis]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|13|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Apollon Limassol FC|Apollon Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Christos Sielis]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|2|2|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[OFI Crete F.C.|OFI]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Konstantinos Sergiou]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|10|2|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Nea Salamis Famagusta FC|Salamina]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Panayiotis Andreou]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|4|29|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Charalampos Charalampous]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|4|4|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=4|club=[[Aris Limassol FC|Aris Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Grigoris Kastanos]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|30|df=y}}|caps=80|goals=9|club=[[Aris Limassol FC|Aris Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ioannis Kosti]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|03|17|df=yes}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Levadiakos F.C.|Levadiakos]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Charalampos Kyriakou]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|2|20|df=y}}|caps=78|goals=0|club=[[AEK Larnaca FC|AEK Larnaca]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Giannis Satsias]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|12|28|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=1|club=[[Panetolikos F.C.|Panetolikos]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Giorgos Naoum]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|4|21|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AEK Larnaca FC|AEK Larnaca]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Andreas Christou (football player|Andreas Christou]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|8|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Stavros Georgiou (footballer born in 2004)|Stavros Georgiou]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|10|19|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Omonia Aradippou]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Andronikos Kakoullis]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|5|3|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=7|club=[[Aris Limassol FC|Aris Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Loizos Loizou]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|7|18|df=y}}|caps=47|goals=3|club=[[Hapoel Tel Aviv F.C.|Hapoel Tel Aviv]]|clubnat=ISR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Angelos Neophytou]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|5|23|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ioannis Pittas]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|7|10|df=y}}|caps=58|goals=11|club=[[PFC CSKA Sofia|CSKA Sofia]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Marinos Tzionis]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|7|16|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=5|club=[[FC UTA Arad|UTA Arad]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Cyprus squad within the last 12 months and are still available for selection.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals and last name.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Panayiotis Kyriakou|age={{birth date and age|2004|5|5|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AEL Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Demetris Demetriou]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|1|15|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Apollon Limassol FC|Apollon Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|ROU}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nikolas Panagiotou]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|5|12|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kostas Pileas]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|12|11|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=1|club=[[Pafos FC|Pafos]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ioannis Kousoulos]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|6|14|df=y}}|caps=50|goals=4|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Rafail Mamas]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|3|4|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AEL Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|ROU}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Nikolas Koutsakos]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|11|14|df=yes}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[APOEL FC|APOEL]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Evangelos Andreou]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|9|24|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|MLD}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Marios Ilia (footballer)|Marios Ilia]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|5|19|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=1|club=[[Pafos FC|Pafos]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|ROU}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Stavros Gavriel]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|29|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[S.V. Zulte Waregem|Zulte Waregem]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|ROU}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
*<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.
*<sup>RET</sup> Retired from international football.
*<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|7 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Cyprus – Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2023-02-02 |archive-date=2022-12-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221227103726/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Cyprus.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Ioannis Okkas1.jpg|175px|right|thumb|[[Ioannis Okkas]] is Cyprus' most capped player with 103 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=30 |Rank
!width=160 |Name
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Goals
!width=80 |Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|[[Ioannis Okkas]]
|103
|27
|1997–2011
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Constantinos Charalambidis]]
|93
|12
|2003–2017
|-
|3
|align="left"|[[Michalis Konstantinou]]
|84
|32
|1997–2012
|-
|4
|align="left"|[[Pambos Pittas]]
|82
|7
|1987–1999
|-
|rowspan="2"|5
|align="left"|'''[[Kostakis Artymatas]]'''
|80
|1
|2012–present
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Grigoris Kastanos]]'''
|80
|9
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan="2"|7
|align="left"|'''[[Charalampos Kyriakou]]'''
|78
|0
|2014–present
|-
|align="left"|[[Constantinos Makrides]]
|77
|5
|2004–2016
|-
|9
|align="left"|[[Nicos Panayiotou]]
|74
|0
|1994–2006
|-
|10
|align="left"|[[Demetris Christofi]]
|72
|9
|2008–2023
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=30 |Rank
!width=160 |Name
!width=50 |Goals
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Ratio
!width=80 |Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Michalis Konstantinou]]
|32
|86
|{{#expr:32/86 round 2}}
|1997–2012
|-
|2
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Ioannis Okkas]]
|27
|103
|{{#expr:27/103 round 2}}
|1997–2011
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Pieros Sotiriou]]'''
|12
|67
|{{#expr:12/67 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Constantinos Charalambidis]]
|12
|93
|{{#expr:12/93 round 2}}
|2003–2017
|-
|5
| style="text-align: left;" |'''[[Ioannis Pittas]]'''
|11
|58
|{{#expr:11/58 round 2}}
|2019–present
|-
|6
| style="text-align:left;" |[[Efstathios Aloneftis]]
|10
|62
|{{#expr:10/95 round 2}}
|2005–2017
|-
|rowspan=3|7
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Marios Agathokleous]]
|9
|38
|{{#expr:9/38 round 2}}
|1994–2003
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Demetris Christofi]]
|9
|72
|{{#expr:9/72 round 2}}
|2008–2023
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Grigoris Kastanos]]'''
|9
|80
|{{#expr:9/80 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=4|10
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Fivos Vrahimis]]
|8
|18
|{{#expr:8/18 round 2}}
|1977–1982
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Milenko Špoljarić]]
|8
|21
|{{#expr:8/21 round 2}}
|1997–2001
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Siniša Gogić]]
|8
|37
|{{#expr:8/37 round 2}}
|1994–1999
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Andreas Sotiriou]]
|8
|39
|{{#expr:8/39 round 2}}
|1991–1999
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===All-time record===
{{Updated|07 June 2026}}

{| class="wikitable" align="center" style="text-align:center"
|-
! Competition
! Played
! Won
! Draw
! Lost
! GF
! GA
|-
| align="left"| [[FIFA World Cup qualification]] || 132 || 18 || 17 || 97 || 102 || 331
|-
| align="left"| [[UEFA European Football Championship|European Championship qualification]] || 122 || 19 || 15 || 88 || 101 || 316
|-
| align="left"| [[Exhibition game|Friendly matches]] || 143 || 40 || 36 || 67 || 157 || 216
|-
| align="left"| [[UEFA Nations League]] || 26 || 6 || 6 || 14 || 17 || 46
|-
! Total || 424 || 83 || 74 || 266 || 378  || 909
|}

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=36|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| {{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6 rowspan=3|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy|1861}} [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]
|-
| {{flagicon|France}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
| {{flagicon|Switzerland|civil}} [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Sweden}} [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
| colspan=8|''Withdrew''
| colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
| {{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=17|''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 2
| 7
|-
| {{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
| 4
| 0
| 0
| 4
| 0
| 19
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
| 6
| 0
| 0
| 6
| 2
| 35
|-
| {{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
| 6
| 1
| 0
| 5
| 1
| 14
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina|1861}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
| 6
| 0
| 0
| 6
| 3
| 24
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
| 8
| 0
| 0
| 8
| 4
| 29
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
| 6
| 0
| 0
| 6
| 3
| 18
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
| 8
| 0
| 1
| 7
| 6
| 20
|-
| {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| 10
| 2
| 1
| 7
| 8
| 18
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 8
| 3
| 1
| 4
| 10
| 15
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 10
| 2
| 2
| 6
| 13
| 31
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 10
| 1
| 1
| 8
| 8
| 20
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 10
| 2
| 3
| 5
| 14
| 16
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 10
| 1
| 2
| 7
| 4
| 15
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 10
| 3
| 1
| 6
| 9
| 18
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 10
| 1
| 2
| 7
| 4
| 21
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 8
| 2
| 2
| 4
| 11
| 11
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} {{flagicon|Argentina}} {{flagicon|Paraguay}} {{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=3|''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=3|''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
| {{flagicon|United Nations}} [[2038 FIFA World Cup|2038]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/20|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!132
!18
!17
!97
!102
!331
|}

===UEFA European Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[UEFA European Championship]] record
!width=1% rowspan=24|
!colspan=6|[[UEFA European Championship qualifying|Qualifying]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|France}} [[1960 European Nations' Cup|1960]]
|colspan=8|''Not a UEFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a UEFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain|1945}} [[1964 European Nations' Cup|1964]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[UEFA Euro 1968|1968]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=15|''Did not qualify''
||6||1||0||5||3||25
|-
|{{flagicon|Belgium}} [[UEFA Euro 1972|1972]]
||6||0||0||6||2||26
|-
|{{flagicon|Yugoslavia}} [[UEFA Euro 1976|1976]]
||6||0||0||6||0||16
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[UEFA Euro 1980|1980]]
||6||0||1||5||2||19
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[UEFA Euro 1984|1984]]
||8||0||2||6||4||21
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[UEFA Euro 1988|1988]]
||8||0||1||7||3||16
|-
|{{flagicon|Sweden}} [[UEFA Euro 1992|1992]]
||8||0||0||8||2||25
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[UEFA Euro 1996|1996]]
||10||1||4||5||6||20
|-
|{{flagicon|Belgium}} {{flagicon|Netherlands}} [[UEFA Euro 2000|2000]]
||8||4||0||4||12||21
|-
|{{flagicon|Portugal}} [[UEFA Euro 2004|2004]]
||8||2||2||4||9||18
|-
|{{flagicon|Austria}} {{flagicon|Switzerland|civil}} [[UEFA Euro 2008|2008]]
||12||4||2||6||17||24
|-
|{{flagicon|Poland}} {{flagicon|Ukraine}} [[UEFA Euro 2012|2012]]
||8||0||2||6||7||20
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]]
||10||4||0||6||16||17
|-
|{{flagicon|European Union}} [[UEFA Euro 2020|2020]]
||10||3||1||6||15||20
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]]
||8||0||0||8||3||28
|-
|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} {{flagicon|Republic of Ireland}} [[UEFA Euro 2028|2028]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''To be determined'' 
|colspan=6 rowspan=3|''To be determined'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} {{flagicon|Turkey}} [[UEFA Euro 2032|2032]]
|-
|{{flagicon|European Union}} [[UEFA Euro 2036|2036]]
|-
!Total||||0/18||||||||||||
!122||19||15||88||101||316
|}

===UEFA Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=11|[[UEFA Nations League]] record
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
!width=28|{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!Rank
|-
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League|2018–19]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League C#Group 3|3]]
| 6
| 1
| 2
| 3
| 5
| 9
| {{same position}}
| 36th
|-
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League|2020–21]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C#Group 1|1]]
| 8
| 2
| 2
| 4
| 4
| 10
| {{same position}}
| 46th
|-
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2]]
| 6
| 1
| 2
| 3
| 4
| 12
| {{same position}}
| 45th
|-
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2]]
| 6
| 2
| 0
| 4
| 4
| 15
| {{same position}}
| 43rd
|-
!colspan=3|Total
!26
!6
!6
!14
!17
!46
!colspan=2|36th
|}

==Head-to-head record==
As of 7 June 2026.

{{legend2|CCFFCC|Positive record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|FFFFCC|Neutral record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|FFDACC|Negative record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{| class="wikitable sortable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center; font-size: 100%;"
!Opponent
!P
!W
!D
!L
|- bgcolor=FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ALB}}
|6
|2
|2
|2
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AND}}
|5
|5
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ARM}}
|7
|4
|2
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AUT}}
|9
|0
|1
|8
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AZE}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BLR}}
|3
|1
|0
|2
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BEL}}
|13
|0
|1
|12
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BIH}}
|6
|2
|1
|3
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BUL}}
|17
|1
|2
|14
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CAN}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CRO}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CZE}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|TCH}}
|6
|0
|2
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|DEN}}
|6
|0
|1
|5
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ENG}}
|2
|0
|0
|2
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|EST}}
|12
|5
|4
|3
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FRO}}
|4
|3
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FIN}}
|4
|1
|1
|2
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FRA}}
|8
|0
|1
|7
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GEO}}
|8
|3
|1
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GER}}
|6
|0
|1
|5
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GIB}}
|2
|2
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GRE}}
|31
|5
|6
|20
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|HUN}}
|8
|1
|0
|7
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ISL}}
|7
|1
|3
|3
|- bgcolor=FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IRN}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IRQ}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ISR}}
|16
|4
|3
|9
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ITA}}
|8
|0
|1
|7
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|JPN}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|JOR}}
|5
|1
|2
|2
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KAZ}}
|4
|3
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KVX}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KUW}}
|2
|1
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LVA}}
|3
|2
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LIB}}
|2
|1
|0
|1
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LIE}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LTU}}
|6
|5
|0
|1
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LUX}}
|5
|4
|0
|1
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MLT}}
|8
|3
|3
|2
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MDA}}
|3
|2
|0
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MNE}}
|5
|0
|3
|2
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NED}}
|8
|0
|0
|8
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MKD}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NIR}}
|8
|1
|4
|3
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NOR}}
|13
|0
|0
|13
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|POL}}
|7
|0
|3
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|POR}}
|11
|0
|1
|10
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IRL}}
|10
|1
|1
|8
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ROU}}
|17
|1
|4
|12
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|RUS}}
|7
|0
|1
|6
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SMR}}
|9
|9
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KSA}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SCO}}
|9
|0
|0
|9
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SER}}
|5
|0
|1
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SVK}}
|6
|1
|1
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SVN}}
|13
|3
|4
|6
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|URS}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ESP}}
|10
|1
|0
|9
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SWE}}
|6
|0
|1
|5
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SUI}}
|8
|1
|2
|5
|- bgcolor=CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SYR}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UKR}}
|4
|1
|1
|2
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|WAL}}
|7
|2
|0
|5
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|YUG}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|- bgcolor=FFDACC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ZAM}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|-
|}

==Honours==
===Friendly===
* '''[[Cyprus International Football Tournament]]'''
** '''Champions (2)''': 1998, 2000

==References==
;Notes
{{reflist|group="note"}}
;Citation
{{Reflist}}
{{reflist|group=note}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* {{official website|http://www.cfa.com.cy/}}
* [https://www.uefa.com/nationalassociations/cyp/ Cyprus] at [[UEFA]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/CYP Cyprus] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cyp-intres.html RSSSF archive of results 1949–]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-recintlp.html RSSSF archive of most capped players and highest goalscorers]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/cyp-coach.html RSSSF archive of coaches 1929–]

{{Cyprus national football team}}
{{Football in Cyprus}}
{{UEFA teams}}
{{National sports teams of Cyprus}}

[[Category:Cyprus national football team| ]]
[[Category:Cyprus men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:Cyprus Football Association national teams| ]]
[[Category:1949 establishments in Cyprus]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1949]]
